title: inula × menesiensis
intro: Inula × menesiensis, also known as the Menzies' elecampane, is a hybrid plant species belonging to the Asteraceae family, resulting from the cross between Inula britannica and Inula helenium. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it effective in treating various health issues. The health benefits of Inula × menesiensis include its potential to alleviate respiratory problems, such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as its ability to soothe digestive issues and reduce inflammation. In herbal preparations, Inula × menesiensis is often used in teas, tinctures, and infusions to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
